content:
# Galatians Chapter 6 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/GAL_006_page_001.jpg

1. Brothers, even if a man is caught in some trespass, you the spiritual [ones] restore such a one in a spirit of gentleness, watching yourself lest you also be tempted.

2. Bear one another's burdens, and so fulfill the law of Christos.

3. For if anyone thinks himself to be something, being nothing, he deceives himself.

4. But let each [one] test his own work, and then he will have [the] boast in himself alone and not in the other.

5. For each [one] will bear his own load.

6. And let the [one] being taught the word share in all good things with the [one] teaching.

7. Do not be deceived: God is not mocked. For whatever a man sows, this also he will reap.

8. Because the [one] sowing to his own flesh will reap from the flesh corruption, but the [one] sowing to the Spirit will reap from the Spirit eternal life.

9. And let us not grow weary in doing the good, for at [the] proper time we will reap, not giving up.

10. So then, as we have opportunity, let us work the good toward all, and especially toward the [ones] of the household of the faith.

11. See with what large letters I have written to you with my own hand.

12. As many as want to make a good appearance in [the] flesh, these compel you to be circumcised — only so that they may not be persecuted for the cross of Christos.

13. For not even the [ones] being circumcised themselves keep [the] law, but they want you to be circumcised so that in your flesh they may boast.

14. But may it never be for me to boast except in the cross of our Lord Yeshua Christos, through whom the world has been crucified to me and I to the world.

15. For neither circumcision is anything nor uncircumcision, but a new creation.

16. And as many as will walk by this rule, peace upon them and mercy, and upon the Yisrael of God.

17. From now on let no one cause me troubles, for I bear on my body the marks of Yeshua.

18. The grace of our Lord Yeshua Christos [be] with your spirit, brothers. Amen.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.2 — "the law of Christos":** ton nomon tou Christou — a striking phrase in a letter arguing against law. Paul distinguishes the "law of Christ" from the Torah — the Torah's essence (love, per 5:14) is fulfilled through mutual burden-bearing.

2. **v.5 — "his own load":** phortion — distinct from baros ("burden") in v.2. baros = heavy, oppressive burden that should be shared; phortion = one's personal pack/load of responsibility. The apparent contradiction between "bear one another's burdens" (v.2) and "each one will bear his own load" (v.5) is resolved by the different Greek words.

3. **v.11 — "with what large letters":** pēlikois grammasin — "with how large letters." This may indicate Paul's poor eyesight (cf. 4:15 — "you would have torn out your eyes"), his emphasis (large letters = shouting on paper), or simply the shift from secretary to Paul's own hand.

4. **v.14 — "the world has been crucified to me":** The reciprocal crucifixion — the world to Paul and Paul to the world — is a metaphor of total mutual death. Paul and the world are dead to each other through the cross.

5. **v.15 — "a new creation":** kainē ktisis — "new creation." This is not merely personal renewal but cosmic transformation. The phrase echoes Isaiah 65:17 ("new heavens and new earth"). In Christ, the old categories (circumcision/uncircumcision) are obsolete because the cosmos itself is renewed.

6. **v.16 — "the Yisrael of God":** ton Israēl tou theou — this phrase is debated: does it refer to ethnic Israel who walk by the Spirit-rule? Or to the church as the new/true Israel? The kai ("and") before "upon the Israel of God" could be epexegetical ("that is, the Israel of God") or additive ("and also upon the Israel of God"). I preserve the ambiguity.

7. **v.17 — "the marks of Yeshua":** ta stigmata tou Iēsou — stigmata were brand marks on slaves or devotees of a deity. Paul's scars from persecution (beatings, stonings) serve as his "brand marks" — he belongs to Jesus, not to any human authority.

— EMET, AI Translator
